hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (on the day of Idal-Adha), "The first thing we will do on this day of ours, is to offer the (`Id) prayer and then return to slaughter the sacrifice. Whoever does so, he acted according to our Sunna (tradition), and whoever slaughtered (the sacrifice) before the prayer, what he offered was just meat he presented to his family, and that will not be considered as Nusak (sacrifice)." (On hearing that) Abu Burda bin Niyar got up, for he had slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer, and said, "I have got a six month old ram." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, 'Slaughter it (as a sacrifice) but it will not be sufficient for any-one else (as a sacrifice after you). Al-Bara' added: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered (the sacrifice) after the prayer, he slaughtered it at the right time and followed the tradition of the Muslims
question: What is the significance of performing the Eid prayer before slaughtering the sacrifice on Eid al-Adha?
verification_reason: The Hadith text explains the significance of performing the Eid prayer before slaughtering the sacrifice on Eid al-Adha, as per the Prophet's (ﷺ) tradition.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer, he just slaughtered it for himself, and whoever slaughtered it after the prayer, he slaughtered it at the right time and followed the tradition of the Muslims
question: What is the significance of timing in performing religious sacrifices in Islam?

I get in my share of Jadha'a (a six month old ram)." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it as a sacrifice

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:that the Prophet (ﷺ) entered upon her when she had her menses at Sarif before entering Mecca, and she was weeping (because she was afraid that she would not be able to perform the Hajj). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "What is wrong with you? Have you got your period?" She said, "Yes." He said, "This is a matter Allah has decreed for all the daughters of Adam, so perform all the ceremonies of Hajj like the others, but do not perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba." `Aisha added: When we were at Mina, beef was brought to me and I asked, "What is this?" They (the people) said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has slaughtered some cows as sacrifices on behalf of his wives
question: What guidance does Islam provide for women who are menstruating during the Hajj pilgrimage?
verification_reason: The answer provides guidance on what a menstruating woman can and cannot do during Hajj, specifically not performing Tawaf around the Ka'ba.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said on the day of Nahr, "Whoever has slaughtered his sacrifice before the prayer, should repeat it (slaughter another sacrifice)." A man got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! This is a day on which meat is desired." He then mentioned his neighbors saying, "I have a six month old ram which is to me better than the meat of two sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed him to slaughter it as a sacrifice, but I do not know whether this permission was valid for other than that man or not. The Prophet (ﷺ) then went towards two rams and slaughtered them, and then the people went towards some sheep and distributed them among themselves
question: What is the significance of performing the sacrifice after the prayer on the day of Nahr?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of performing the sacrifice after the prayer on the day of Nahr, and provides a specific example of the Prophet's permission to slaughter a six month old ram.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Bakra:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Time has come back to its original state which it had on the day Allah created the Heavens and the Earth. The year is twelve months, four of which are sacred, three of them are in succession, namely Dhul-Qa'da, Dhul Hijja and Muharram, (the fourth being) Rajab Mudar which is between Juma'da (ath-thamj and Sha'ban. The Prophet (ﷺ) then asked, "Which month is this?" We said, "Allah and his Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it by a name other than its real name. He said, "Isn't it the month of Dhul-Hijja?" We said, "Yes." He said, "Which town is this?" We said, "Allah and His Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it t,y a name other than its real name. He said, "isn't it the town (of Mecca)?" We replied, "Yes." He said, "What day is today?" We replied, "Allah and His Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it by a name other than its real name. He said, "Isn't it the day of Nahr?" We replied, "Yes." He then said, "Your blood, properties and honor are as sacred to one another as this day of yours in this town of yours in this month of yours. You will meet your Lord, and He will ask you about your deeds. Beware! Do not go astray after me by cutting the necks of each other. It is incumbent upon those who are present to convey this message to those who are absent, for some of those to whom it is conveyed may comprehend it better than some of those who have heard it directly." (Muhammad, the sub-narrator, on mentioning this used to say: The Prophet then said, "No doubt! Haven't I delivered (Allah's) Message (to you)? Haven't I delivered Allah's message (to you)?
question: What is the significance of the sacred months in Islam and which months are considered sacred?
verification_reason: The answer correctly explains the significance of the sacred months and identifies the four sacred months in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib:An uncle of mine called Abu Burda, slaughtered his sacrifice before the `Id prayer. So Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to him, "Your (slaughtered) sheep was just mutton (not a sacrifice)." Abu Burda said, "O Allah's Apostle! I have got a domestic kid goat." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it (as a sacrifice) but it will not be permissible for anybody other than you" The Prophet (ﷺ) added, "Whoever slaughtered his sacrifice before the (`Id) prayer, he only slaughtered for himself, and whoever slaughtered it after the prayer, he offered his sacrifice properly and followed the tradition of the Muslims

He said, "This is a matter Allah has decreed for all the daughters of Adam, so perform the ceremonies of the Hajj as the pilgrims do, but do not perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) slaughtered some cows as sacrifices on behalf of his wives

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) delivering a sermon, and he said (on the Day of `Id-Allah. a), "The first thing we will do on this day of ours is that we will offer the `Id prayer, then we will return and slaughter our sacrifices; and whoever does so, then indeed he has followed our tradition, and whoever slaughtered his sacrifice (before the prayer), what he offered was just meat that he presented to his family, and that was not a sacrifice." Abu Burda got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer and I have got a Jadha'a which is better than an old sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it to make up for that, but it will not be sufficient for anybody else after you
question: What is the significance of performing the `Id prayer before slaughtering the sacrifice on the day of `Id?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of performing the `Id prayer before slaughtering the sacrifice on the day of `Id, as per the Prophet's tradition.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered the sacrifice before the `Id prayer, should repeat it (slaughter another one)." A man said "This is the day on which meat is desired." Then he mentioned the need of his neighbors (for meat) and the Prophet (ﷺ) seemed to accept his excuse. The man said, "I have a Jadha'a which is to me better than two sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed him (to slaughter it as a sacrifice). But I do not know whether this permission was general for all Muslims or not. The Prophet (ﷺ) then went towards two rams and slaughtered them, and the people went towards their sheep and slaughtered them
question: What is the significance of performing the sacrifice after the Eid prayer in Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of performing the sacrifice after the Eid prayer, specifically mentioning the Prophet's instruction to repeat the sacrifice if done before the prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Masruq:that he came to `Aisha and said to her, "O Mother of the Believers! There is a man who sends a Hadi to Ka`ba and stays in his city and requests that his Hadi camel be garlanded while he remains in a state of Ihram from that day till the people finish their Ihram (after completing all the ceremonies of Hajj)" (What do you say about it?) Masruq added, I heard the clapping of her hands behind the curtain. She said, "I used to twist the garlands for the Hadi of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and he used to send his Hadi to Ka`ba but he never used to regard as unlawful what was lawful for men to do with their wives till the people returned (from the Hajj)
question: What is the significance of sending a Hadi to the Ka'ba in Islamic tradition?

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:During the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) we used to take with us the meat of the sacrifices (of Id al Adha) to Medina. (The narrator often said. The meat of the Hadi)
question: What was the practice regarding the meat of sacrifices during the time of the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)?

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:that once he was not present (at the time of `Id-al-Adha) and when he came. some meat was presented to him. and the people said (to him), 'This is the meat of our sacrifices" He said. 'Take it away; I shall not taste it. (In his narration) Abu Sa`id added: I got up and went to my brother, Abu Qatada (who was his maternal brother and was one of the warriors of the battle of Badr) and mentioned that to him He Sa`d. 'A new verdict was given in your absence (i.e., meat of sacrifices was allowed to be stored and eaten later on)
question: What was the initial stance on consuming the meat of sacrifices after Eid al-Adha?
verification_reason: The answer explains the initial stance on consuming the meat of sacrifices after Eid al-Adha as being disallowed, and the change in verdict after the Prophet's return.

hadith-eng: Narrated Salama bin Al-Aqua':The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever has slaughtered a sacrifice should not keep anything of Its meat after three days." When it was the next year the people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Shall we do as we did last year?" He said, ' Eat of it and feed of it to others and store of it for in that year the people were having a hard time and I wanted you to help (the needy)
question: What is the significance of distributing meat from a sacrifice during times of hardship?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of distributing meat from a sacrifice during times of hardship, as per the Prophet's instruction.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:We used to salt some of the meat of sacrifice and present it to the Prophet (ﷺ) at Medina. Once he said, "Do not eat (of that meat) for more than three days." That was not a final order, but (that year) he wanted us to feed of it to others, Allah knows better
question: What is the significance of distributing sacrificial meat to others in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of distributing sacrificial meat to others by mentioning the Prophet's (ﷺ) instruction to feed others with it.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:the freed slave of Ibn Azhar that he witnessed the Day of `Id-al-Adha with `Umar bin Al-Khattab. `Umar offered the `Id prayer before the sermon and then delivered the sermon before the people, saying, "O people! Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has forbidden you to fast (on the first day of) each of these two 'Ida, for one of them is the Day of breaking your fast, and the other is the one, on which you eat the meat of your sacrifices

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:(in continuation of above). Then I witnessed the `Id with `Uthman bin `Affan, and that was on a Friday. He offered the prayer before the sermon, saying, "O people! Today you have two 'Its (festivals) together, so whoever of those who live at Al-`Awali (suburbs) would like to wait for the Jumua prayer, he may wait, and whoever would like to return (home) Is granted my permission to do so

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:(in continuation of above). hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) delivering a sermon, and he said (on the Day of `Id-Allah. a), "The first thing we will do on this day of ours is that we will offer the `Id prayer, then we will return and slaughter our sacrifices; and whoever does so, then indeed he has followed our tradition, and whoever slaughtered his sacrifice (before the prayer), what he offered was just meat that he presented to his family, and that was not a sacrifice." Abu Burda got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer and I have got a Jadha'a which is better than an old sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it to make up for that, but it will not be sufficient for anybody else after you
question: How does the timing of the sacrifice affect its validity according to Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: The text explains that the sacrifice should be done after the prayer, and if done before, it is considered just meat and not a valid sacrifice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered the sacrifice before the `Id prayer, should repeat it (slaughter another one)." A man said "This is the day on which meat is desired." Then he mentioned the need of his neighbors (for meat) and the Prophet (ﷺ) seemed to accept his excuse. The man said, "I have a Jadha'a which is to me better than two sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed him (to slaughter it as a sacrifice). But I do not know whether this permission was general for all Muslims or not. The Prophet (ﷺ) then went towards two rams and slaughtered them, and the people went towards their sheep and slaughtered them
question: How does the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) demonstrate flexibility and understanding in addressing the needs of his community?
verification_reason: The answer demonstrates the Prophet's flexibility and understanding in addressing the needs of his community by accepting the man's excuse and allowing him to slaughter his Jadha'a as a sacrifice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jundab bin Sufyan Al-Bajali:I witnessed the Prophet (ﷺ) on the Day of Nahr. He said, "Whoever slaughtered the sacrifice before offering the `Id prayer, should slaughter another sacrifice in its place; and whoever has not slaughtered their sacrifice yet, should slaughter now
question: What should a person do if they have slaughtered their sacrifice before the `Id prayer?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a solution for the scenario described.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':One day Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) offered the `Id prayer and said, "Whoever offers our prayer and faces our Qibla should not slaughter the sacrifice till he finishes the `Id prayer." Abu Burda bin Niyar got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I have already done it. The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "That is something you have done before its due time." Abu Burda said, "I have a Jadha'a which is better than two old sheep; shall I slaughter it?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Yes, but it will not be sufficient for anyone after you
question: How does the timing of a religious act affect its validity in Islamic practice?
verification_reason: The text explains how the timing of a religious act (sacrificing an animal) affects its validity in Islamic practice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer as sacrifices, two horned rams, black and white in color, and used to put his foot on their sides and slaughter them with his own hands
question: How did the Prophet (ﷺ) perform the act of sacrifice during Eid?
verification_reason: The answer describes the Prophet's (ﷺ) method of performing the sacrifice during Eid.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) offered as sacrifices, two horned rams, black and white in color. He slaughtered them with his own hands and mentioned Allah's Name over them and said Takbir and put his foot on their sides
question: What are the recommended practices and phrases to be recited during the act of animal sacrifice in Islam?
verification_reason: The text describes the Prophet's actions during animal sacrifice, including mentioning Allah's Name, saying Takbir, and putting his foot on the sides of the animal.

hadith-eng: Narrated Masruq:that he came to `Aisha and said to her, "O Mother of the Believers! There is a man who sends a Hadi to Ka`ba and stays in his city and requests that his Hadi camel be garlanded while he remains in a state of Ihram from that day till the people finish their Ihram (after completing all the ceremonies of Hajj)" (What do you say about it?) Masruq added, I heard the clapping of her hands behind the curtain. She said, "I used to twist the garlands for the Hadi of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and he used to send his Hadi to Ka`ba but he never used to regard as unlawful what was lawful for men to do with their wives till the people returned (from the Hajj)
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) approach the state of Ihram when sending a Hadi?
verification_reason: The answer explains the Prophet's approach to Ihram when sending a Hadi, addressing the question directly.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:During the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) we used to take with us the meat of the sacrifices (of Id al Adha) to Medina. (The narrator often said. The meat of the Hadi)
question: How did the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) handle the meat of sacrifices when traveling to Medina?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about how the companions handled the meat of sacrifices when traveling to Medina.

hadith-eng: Narrated Salama bin Al-Aqua':The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever has slaughtered a sacrifice should not keep anything of Its meat after three days." When it was the next year the people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Shall we do as we did last year?" He said, ' Eat of it and feed of it to others and store of it for in that year the people were having a hard time and I wanted you to help (the needy)
question: How does the practice of storing and sharing sacrificial meat reflect on community support and charity?
verification_reason: The answer explains the purpose of storing and sharing sacrificial meat, which is to help the needy during hard times.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:We used to salt some of the meat of sacrifice and present it to the Prophet (ﷺ) at Medina. Once he said, "Do not eat (of that meat) for more than three days." That was not a final order, but (that year) he wanted us to feed of it to others, Allah knows better
question: Why might there be a restriction on consuming sacrificial meat for more than three days?
verification_reason: The text explains the reason behind the restriction on consuming sacrificial meat for more than three days, which is to feed others.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:the freed slave of Ibn Azhar that he witnessed the Day of `Id-al-Adha with `Umar bin Al-Khattab. `Umar offered the `Id prayer before the sermon and then delivered the sermon before the people, saying, "O people! Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has forbidden you to fast (on the first day of) each of these two 'Ida, for one of them is the Day of breaking your fast, and the other is the one, on which you eat the meat of your sacrifices
question: Why is fasting forbidden on the day of Eid al-Adha?
verification_reason: The answer directly states that fasting is forbidden on the day of Eid al-Adha, citing the reason as the day of breaking the fast and eating the meat of sacrifices.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:(in continuation of above). Then I witnessed the `Id with `Uthman bin `Affan, and that was on a Friday. He offered the prayer before the sermon, saying, "O people! Today you have two 'Its (festivals) together, so whoever of those who live at Al-`Awali (suburbs) would like to wait for the Jumua prayer, he may wait, and whoever would like to return (home) Is granted my permission to do so
question: What options are available for people living in the suburbs regarding attending the Friday prayer when it coincides with Eid?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear option for people living in the suburbs regarding attending the Friday prayer when it coincides with Eid.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu `Ubaid:(in continuation of above). Then I witnessed (the 'Its) with `Ali bin Abi Talib, and he too offered the `Id prayer before the sermon and then delivered the sermon before the people and said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has forbidden you to eat the meat of your sacrifices for more than three days
question: What guidance did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) provide regarding the consumption of sacrificial meat during Eid?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the Prophet's guidance on consuming sacrificial meat during Eid.

hadith-eng: Narrated Salim:`Abdullah bin `Umar said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Eat of the meat of sacrifices (of `Id al Adha) for three days." When `Abdullah departed from Mina, he used to eat (bread with) oil, lest he should eat of the meat of Hadi (which is regarded as unlawful after the three days of the `Id)
question: Why did `Abdullah bin `Umar avoid eating the meat of Hadi after three days of `Id al Adha?
verification_reason: The answer explains the reason behind Abdullah bin Umar's action, which is in response to the Prophet's instruction to eat the meat of sacrifices for three days.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (on the day of Idal-Adha), "The first thing we will do on this day of ours, is to offer the (`Id) prayer and then return to slaughter the sacrifice. Whoever does so, he acted according to our Sunna (tradition), and whoever slaughtered (the sacrifice) before the prayer, what he offered was just meat he presented to his family, and that will not be considered as Nusak (sacrifice)." (On hearing that) Abu Burda bin Niyar got up, for he had slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer, and said, "I have got a six month old ram." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, 'Slaughter it (as a sacrifice) but it will not be sufficient for any-one else (as a sacrifice after you). Al-Bara' added: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered (the sacrifice) after the prayer, he slaughtered it at the right time and followed the tradition of the Muslims
question: What are the consequences of slaughtering the sacrifice before the Eid prayer?
verification_reason: The answer clearly states the consequences of slaughtering the sacrifice before the Eid prayer, which is that it will not be considered as a valid sacrifice (Nusak).

hadith-eng: Narrated `Uqba bin 'Amir Al-Juhani:that the Prophet (ﷺ) distributed among his companions some animals for sacrifice (to be slaughtered on `Id-al-Adha). `Uqba's share was a Jadha'a (a six month old goat). `Uqba said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I get in my share of Jadha'a (a six month old ram)." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it as a sacrifice
question: How did the Prophet (ﷺ) address concerns regarding the suitability of sacrificial animals?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the Prophet's (ﷺ) actions regarding sacrificial animals.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:that the Prophet (ﷺ) entered upon her when she had her menses at Sarif before entering Mecca, and she was weeping (because she was afraid that she would not be able to perform the Hajj). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "What is wrong with you? Have you got your period?" She said, "Yes." He said, "This is a matter Allah has decreed for all the daughters of Adam, so perform all the ceremonies of Hajj like the others, but do not perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba." `Aisha added: When we were at Mina, beef was brought to me and I asked, "What is this?" They (the people) said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has slaughtered some cows as sacrifices on behalf of his wives
question: What specific Hajj rituals are women exempt from during their menstrual period according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The Hadith text explicitly states that women with their menstrual period are exempt from performing Tawaf around the Ka'ba during Hajj.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said on the day of Nahr, "Whoever has slaughtered his sacrifice before the prayer, should repeat it (slaughter another sacrifice)." A man got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! This is a day on which meat is desired." He then mentioned his neighbors saying, "I have a six month old ram which is to me better than the meat of two sheep." The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed him to slaughter it as a sacrifice, but I do not know whether this permission was valid for other than that man or not. The Prophet (ﷺ) then went towards two rams and slaughtered them, and then the people went towards some sheep and distributed them among themselves
question: What can be learned about the distribution of meat during the sacrifice from this event?
verification_reason: The text explains that the Prophet allowed a man to slaughter a six-month-old ram as a sacrifice, indicating that the distribution of meat can be from animals of varying ages.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Bakra:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Time has come back to its original state which it had on the day Allah created the Heavens and the Earth. The year is twelve months, four of which are sacred, three of them are in succession, namely Dhul-Qa'da, Dhul Hijja and Muharram, (the fourth being) Rajab Mudar which is between Juma'da (ath-thamj and Sha'ban. The Prophet (ﷺ) then asked, "Which month is this?" We said, "Allah and his Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it by a name other than its real name. He said, "Isn't it the month of Dhul-Hijja?" We said, "Yes." He said, "Which town is this?" We said, "Allah and His Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it t,y a name other than its real name. He said, "isn't it the town (of Mecca)?" We replied, "Yes." He said, "What day is today?" We replied, "Allah and His Apostle know better." He kept silent so long that we thought that he would call it by a name other than its real name. He said, "Isn't it the day of Nahr?" We replied, "Yes." He then said, "Your blood, properties and honor are as sacred to one another as this day of yours in this town of yours in this month of yours. You will meet your Lord, and He will ask you about your deeds. Beware! Do not go astray after me by cutting the necks of each other. It is incumbent upon those who are present to convey this message to those who are absent, for some of those to whom it is conveyed may comprehend it better than some of those who have heard it directly." (Muhammad, the sub-narrator, on mentioning this used to say: The Prophet then said, "No doubt! Haven't I delivered (Allah's) Message (to you)? Haven't I delivered Allah's message (to you)?
question: What responsibility do Muslims have in conveying the message of Islam to others according to the teachings of the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)?
verification_reason: The answer text explicitly mentions the responsibility of Muslims to convey the message of Islam to others.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib:An uncle of mine called Abu Burda, slaughtered his sacrifice before the `Id prayer. So Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to him, "Your (slaughtered) sheep was just mutton (not a sacrifice)." Abu Burda said, "O Allah's Apostle! I have got a domestic kid goat." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Slaughter it (as a sacrifice) but it will not be permissible for anybody other than you" The Prophet (ﷺ) added, "Whoever slaughtered his sacrifice before the (`Id) prayer, he only slaughtered for himself, and whoever slaughtered it after the prayer, he offered his sacrifice properly and followed the tradition of the Muslims
question: What exception did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) make for Abu Burda regarding the sacrifice, and why was it unique to him?
verification_reason: The answer explains the exception made by the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) for Abu Burda regarding the sacrifice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':Abu Burda slaughtered (the sacrifice) before the (`Id) prayer whereupon the Prophet (ﷺ) said to him, "Slaughter another sacrifice instead of that." Abu Burda said, "I have nothing except a Jadha'a." (Shu`ba said: Perhaps Abu Burda also said that Jadha'a was better than an old sheep in his opinion.) The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "(Never mind), slaughter it to make up for the other one, but it will not be sufficient for anyone else after you
question: What exceptions did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) make regarding the type of animal used for sacrifice?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the Prophet's exception regarding the type of animal used for sacrifice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':Abu Burda slaughtered (the sacrifice) before the (`Id) prayer whereupon the Prophet (ﷺ) said to him, "Slaughter another sacrifice instead of that." Abu Burda said, "I have nothing except a Jadha'a." (Shu`ba said: Perhaps Abu Burda also said that Jadha'a was better than an old sheep in his opinion.) The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "(Never mind), slaughter it to make up for the other one, but it will not be sufficient for anyone else after you
question: What is the significance of performing the sacrifice after the Eid prayer in Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:that once he was not present (at the time of `Id-al-Adha) and when he came. some meat was presented to him. and the people said (to him), 'This is the meat of our sacrifices" He said. 'Take it away; I shall not taste it. (In his narration) Abu Sa`id added: I got up and went to my brother, Abu Qatada (who was his maternal brother and was one of the warriors of the battle of Badr) and mentioned that to him He Sa`d. 'A new verdict was given in your absence (i.e., meat of sacrifices was allowed to be stored and eaten later on)
question: How did the verdict regarding the consumption of sacrificial meat change over time?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever slaughtered the sacrifice before the prayer, he just slaughtered it for himself, and whoever slaughtered it after the prayer, he slaughtered it at the right time and followed the tradition of the Muslims
question: What are the consequences of performing a religious act at the incorrect time?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.

hadith-eng: Narrated Nafi': 'Abdullah (bin 'Umar) used to slaughter his sacrifice at the slaughtering place (i.e the slaughtering place of the Prophet (ﷺ)
question: What can be learned from the actions of the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) regarding the performance of religious duties?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer two rams as sacrifices, and I also used to offer two rams
question: What can be learned from the practice of offering two rams as sacrifices?
